feed.title: Docket updates for X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
feed.rights: Created for the public domain by Free Law Project

entry.title: Entry #277 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: NOTICE received from the Supreme Court of Singapore Legal Registry re: 130 Order, 99 Order, 100 Order, 113 Order (saw) (Entered: 05/18/2026)

entry.title: Entry #276 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: Notice of Filing of Official Electronic Transcript of Motions to Compel (172, 178, 195) Proceedings held on 05/13/2026 before Judge Hal R. Ray, Jr.. Court Reporter/Transcriber Kathy Rehling, kathyrehlingtranscripts@gmail.com, Telephone number 972-786-3063. Parties are notified of their duty to review the transcript. A copy may be purchased from the court reporter or viewed at the clerk's office. If the transcript contains personal identifiers that must be redacted under MO 61, Fed.R.Civ.P. 5.2 or Fed.R.Crim.P. 49.1, or if the transcript contains the name of a minor child victim or a minor child witness that must be redacted under 18 U.S.C. § 3509, file a Redaction Request - Transcript within 21 days. If no action is taken, the entire transcript will be made available through PACER without redaction after 90 calendar days. The clerk will mail a copy of this notice to parties not electronically noticed. (58 pages). Redaction Request due 6/4/2026. Redacted Transcript Deadline set for 6/15/2026. Release of Transcript Restriction set for 8/12/2026. (Rehling, Kathy) (Entered: 05/14/2026)

entry.title: Entry #275 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: Transcript Order Form: transcript requested by Apple Inc for Motions to Compel Hearing, Judge Ray, 5/13/26 (Court Reporter: Digital File.) Payment method: Private funds - Requester has obtained the estimate from the reporter and has paid or will pay the cost as directed. Reminder: If the transcript is ordered for an appeal, Appellant must also file a copy of the order form with the appeals court.. (Wisenberg, Julia) (Entered: 05/14/2026)

entry.title: Entry #274 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: ORDER: After considering the pleadings, arguments of counsel, applicable legal authorities, and for the reasons stated at the hearing and the additional reasons explained below, the Court finds that the remaining live Motions (ECF Nos. 172, 195 ) should be and are hereby GRANTED in part. While Apple must still produce the AI policies themselves, the Court DENIES the Motion to Compel production 178 of all documents relating to these policies. (Ordered by Magistrate Judge Hal R. Ray, Jr. on 5/13/2026) (wxc) (Entered: 05/13/2026)

entry.title: Entry #273 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: Transcript Order Form: transcript requested by X Corp, X.AI LLC for Hearing held 5/13/26 (Court Reporter: Digital File.) Payment method: Private funds - Requester has obtained the estimate from the reporter and has paid or will pay the cost as directed. Reminder: If the transcript is ordered for an appeal, Appellant must also file a copy of the order form with the appeals court.. (Fillmore, Charles) (Entered: 05/13/2026)

entry.title: Entry #272 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: ELECTRONIC Minute Entry for proceedings held before Magistrate Judge Hal R. Ray, Jr: Motion Hearing held on 5/13/2026 re: 178 MOTION to Compel Discovery from the OpenAI Defendants and for Expedited Briefing, 195 MOTION to Compel Discovery from Apple and for Expedited Briefing, 172 MOTION to Compel Plaintiffs to Produce Documents; Hearing concluded. Attorney Appearances: Plaintiff - Eva Young, Scott Eisman, Alexander Mark Dvorscak; Defense - Defendant Open AI, Inc, - Stephen Di Prima, Graham W Meli, Ralph H Duggins; Defendant Apple - Dee Kelly, Jr., Henry Liu. (Court Reporter: Digital File) (No exhibits) Time in Court - 1:18. (mcrd) (Entered: 05/13/2026)

entry.title: Entry #271 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: NOTICE of Resolution of Certain Issues from Pending Motions to Compel re: 270 Status Report, 203 Order Setting Deadline/Hearing, filed by OpenAI OpCo, LLC, OpenAI, Inc., OpenAI, L.L.C. (Fredricks, Scott) (Entered: 05/12/2026)

entry.title: Entry #270 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914

entry.title: Entry #267 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: ORDER: Noting that the Motion is unopposed and for good cause shown, the Court GRANTS the Motion (ECF No. 263 ). (Clerk to enter the sealed and/or ex parte document as of the date of this order.) (Ordered by Magistrate Judge Hal R. Ray, Jr on 5/11/2026) (saw) (Entered: 05/11/2026)

entry.title: Entry #266 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: ORDER: Before the Court is the Motion to Seal. ECF No. 263 . The Court hereby ORDERS that the Motion is REFERRED to Judge Ray. Motion referred to Magistrate Judge Hal R. Ray, Jr. (Ordered by Judge Mark Pittman on 5/11/2026) (saw) (Entered: 05/11/2026)

entry.title: Entry #253 in X Corp. v. Apple Inc., 4:25-cv-00914
entry.summary: ORDER: Before the Court is the Motion for Leave to Take Additional Depositions. ECF No. 191 . The Court hereby ORDERS that the Motion is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The Court also hereby ORDERS that the Motion for Protective Order filed by Defendant, Apple, Inc. is MOOT. ECF No. 221 . (Ordered by Judge Mark Pittman on 4/30/2026) (saw) (Entered: 04/30/2026)
